summary: A fortified rectangular building with three tall corner towers and a recessed central block. Walls are primarily gray stone-like blocks with narrow horizontal windows, a raised parapet and flat overhanging tower roofs with dark red tops. The structure sits on a raised stone foundation with evenly spaced stone block buttresses and a surrounding cleared dirt perimeter.

shape.overall_form: rectangular central hall with three tall square towers at three corners and a crenellated parapet; central roof area is large and mostly flat
shape.symmetry: approximate bilateral symmetry along front-back axis (towers not perfectly symmetric: two tall towers to one side and one to the other)

materials.name: fence
materials.role: small rooftop posts / decorative lights (possible fence or iron bars)

materials.name: wood
materials.role: uncertain - possibly door frames or interior support

elements: narrow horizontal windows arranged in rows on the main block
three tall square towers with overhanging flat roofs
stone block buttresses / base plinth around perimeter
raised central roof / courtyard area with dark red floor
entrance at front center with small stairs / recessed doorway
rebuild_hints: Main hall: rectangular volume approx 25x20 blocks with three stacked rows of 1x3 horizontal windows centered on the front face.

Towers: three square towers (approx 4x4 footprint) rising ~16-18 blocks above foundation; place two towers toward the rear corners and one at front-left corner as seen; top with 1-2 block overhang of stone slabs and a dark red 7x7 flat roof (nether brick or red material).
Foundation: one-block high stone plinth around perimeter with evenly spaced 1-block protruding buttresses (stone blocks) every 2-3 blocks.
Roof: central roof is flat and covered in dark red material with a low stone slab parapet around it and occasional glow block/lantern on corners.
Windows: use glass panes or glass blocks in groups of three horizontally, repeated on each floor; towers have single small windows stacked vertically.
uncertainties: Exact block type for dark red roof is uncertain (looks like nether brick or red nether material) - labeled as 'brick' with moderate confidence.
Small decorative posts on tower roofs might be fence posts, glowstone, or torches; identification is uncertain.
Internal floor counts and interior layout are not visible; floors_estimate is inferred from window rows and tower height.
Precise footprint dimensions and spacing between towers are approximate from aerial views.
